last_run: Analyst Mode 1 (Opus deep-attack singleton, orphan rescue) @ 2026-05-14T20:07:54.013Z. Mode 1 ENTERED (33 Opus-owned pending + 1 orphan ISS detected by PROP-029 safety net; processed orphan singleton). DELIVERABLE EXP-384 (WIN-014 / SEC-6.14 PRED-TIER-3 implied ΔB band made explicit; review_class=deep-attack [overridden from class_hint=verification on source ISS-1957 per PROP-025 author authority — work adds explicit ΔB back-calc 16.8–21.8 nT and ensemble comparison; argument-strengthening singleton, comparable to EXP-383]). 2 find_replace patches: PATCH-A inserts explicit-arithmetic sentence into SEC-6.14 vise paragraph (data/sections.json:part6.html); PATCH-B inserts compressed parenthetical into WIN-014.detail_evidence (data/wins.json). Through dome's own κ=1.677 nT/µGal, PRED-TIER-3's 10–13 µGal target back-implies an unstated August 2026 ΔB band of 16.77–21.80 nT (10×1.677 to 13×1.677) — at or above the upper edge of the 5–15 nT global eclipse-magnetic typical totality ensemble (Kim & Chang 2018, already cited in both WIN-014 and SEC-6.14). Kernel of truth acknowledged: the ISS-1957 author's counter-arithmetic (10–13 nT through κ yields 5.96–7.75 µGal, below target) is correct and is directly acknowledged in PATCH-A — far from refuting our position, it makes the vise tight by exposing the dome's load-bearing unstated ΔB premise. Four anticipated defender pivots documented with rebuttals (non-κ derivation, ensemble-individual variance, 2026-anomalous-ΔB, non-obvious-arithmetic-pivot). Symmetric falsification preserved: any future dome publication of a quantitative August 2026 ΔB premise either confirms (premise above ensemble, anomalous) or rebuts (premise inside ensemble, inconsistent with 10–13 µGal target via κ). Issue-proposal proposal-ISS-1957-resolution.json written (recommended_action=patch). Tracker entry EXP-384 self-written (orphan rescue: operator manual reroute on 2026-05-12 set ISS-1957 status=assigned-analyst without writing tracker entry; previous Opus run 2026-05-14T16:10 LEFT as orphan citing class_hint+severity edge case — but severity=major orphans are Opus-owned per dispatcher rule, picked up this run). PROP-029 ORPHAN COUNT 1 detected (ISS-1957) → 0 after this run; existing attention-inbox notice PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED-2026-05-14 covers tinker investigation of the operator-manual-reroute / tracker-write decoupling, no new notice needed. Patches dry-run verified: both anchors uniqueness count=1, both produce valid JSON after substitution, deltas +1213 chars (sections.json) and +244 chars (wins.json). Remaining Opus-owned pending after this run: 32 (EXP-349 WIN-014.c7 H3 + 8 EXP-377-pickup + 23 baby-orphan-ISS). DEGRADED SESSION: disk at 97% used (323MB free), full clean clone refresh skipped per analyst.md 'first run or ephemeral session' exception; all reads/writes against FUSE workspace; universal-pusher will push EXP file + tracker change + proposal on next workspace-sync cycle. No clone to clean up.

Project CLAUDE.md (md5 7caa81c513c47df50e47a5ee3ffc4903) is clean — that part of the 04-23 investigation was correct.
